minutes_briefs.title: Clean Energy Act deadline extended to April 1, 2029
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC government has pushed back a key deadline under the Clean Energy Act. A regulation that was set to expire or require renewal on April 1, 2026, will now stay in effect until April 1, 2029. This gives businesses an extra three years before they need to deal with whatever that rule requires — like meeting a new standard, filing a report, or getting a permit. The change was made by the Treasury Board (the government committee that manages spending and regulations) and was published on March 24, 2026.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action is needed — the deadline extension gives you until April 1, 2029 to comply.

key_dates.label: Clean Energy Act regulation deadline extension takes effect

minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 29/2026
Made under: Clean Energy Act
Published in: No. 5 – B.C. Regs. 29 to 42, March 24, 2026
Deposit date: March 11, 2026

B.C. Reg. 29/2026, deposited March 11, 2026, under theCLEAN ENERGY ACT[section 38 (b)]. Regulation of Treasury Board, dated March 10, 2026. Treasury Board orders that B.C. Reg. 185/2023 is amended in paragraphs (c) and (d) by striking out "April 1, 2026" and substituting "April 1, 2029". — B. BAILEY,Chair, Treasury Board.
